response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an accomplished full-stack and web engineer with 15+ years of experience, but is a poor fit for this role. The most disqualifying factor is the complete absence of Japanese language proficiency, which is listed as a hard requirement. Beyond that, the resume shows no security domain expertise (MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption protocols), no embedded networking or LAN-switch experience, and only superficial C/C++ usage with no evidence of Linux kernel-level development. The candidate's strengths lie entirely outside the required technical and linguistic scope of this position.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across multiple languages and domains
Some embedded and hardware-adjacent work (NI LabWindows C/C++, robotic arms, hardware control at Altom)
Familiarity with Linux environments and low-latency systems development (Sociomantic, DLang async I/O)
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency whatsoever — a stated hard requirement for this role
Zero security domain expertise: no MACsec, 802.1X, Captive Portal, encryption protocols, or attack/tamper detection
No embedded networking, LAN-switch, Linux kernel modification, or chip-vendor SDK experience

key_factors.factor: No embedded networking or kernel experience
